soccerCoach_teamNotes.Taylor:
SEASON NOTES – 2025

G5 vs Toowong White (W 3–1): Excellent game, one of our most dangerous players going forward. Won the ball deeper and drove into attack, frequently beat defenders on the outside with pace. Key focus area: decision-making and distribution after beating players — "beat one, then distribute."

G6 vs Mitchelton Glitter (W 1–0): Strong game on the right wing, consistently made ground down the flank and created scoring opportunities. Scored a well-earned goal, unlucky not to add to her tally after two other close chances.

G7 vs Sapphires (D 4–4): Difficult first half, sitting too deep and not getting involved in attacking play. Responded strongly in the second half — pushed higher, became more involved and scored an important goal. Her second-half performance showed the difference she can make when she plays aggressively.

G9 vs Springfield (W 6–3): Scored a goal and provided strong attacking support. Continued to improve her movement and positioning in advanced areas.

G10 vs Bardon Bandits (W 3–2): Strong performance especially in the first half. Several excellent runs down the wing, opened the scoring. Delivered several dangerous balls across the face of goal. First half stronger than second. Focus: poachers need to be in position to convert those crosses.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Release the ball earlier after winning space. "Beat one, then distribute." Continue pushing high in attack from the start, not just in the second half.
soccerCoach_teamNotes.Sydney:
SEASON NOTES – 2025

G5 vs Toowong White (W 3–1): Solid performance, worked hard throughout, contributed well offensively and scored a goal. Reliable effort and involvement across the field.

G6 vs Mitchelton Glitter (W 1–0): Not quite at usual level but still contributed. Won the ball effectively but didn't always follow up with the second effort needed to create further opportunities.

G7 vs Sapphires (D 4–4): Grew into the game and became more effective as the match progressed.

G9 vs Springfield (W 6–3): Outstanding attacking performance. Scored two goals and remained a constant threat. Used pace well and made intelligent attacking runs.

G10 vs Bardon Bandits (W 3–2): Another dependable performance through midfield. Linked play well, made several strong runs, kept the team moving forward.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Second efforts after winning the ball — follow up immediately with pressure and support. Continue developing as an attacking runner.
soccerCoach_teamNotes.Pippa:
SEASON NOTES – 2025

G5 vs Toowong White (W 3–1): Good strong game, adapted reasonably well to playing left wing (not usual position). Scored a goal, worked hard on and off the ball.

G6 vs Mitchelton Glitter (W 1–0): Worked hard on the left wing, made some good forward runs. Occasionally found herself shepherded towards the sideline and struggled to bring the ball back into central areas.

G7 vs Sapphires (D 4–4): One of her strongest games of the season. Scored two goals playing striker. Looked dangerous whenever she got forward — well-earned double.

G9 vs Springfield (W 6–3): Worked hard up front and contributed an important goal. Continued to pressure defenders and create opportunities.

G10 vs Bardon Bandits (W 3–2): Scored a goal (capped off the game). Continues to find dangerous attacking positions. Next step: make decisions a touch earlier — on several occasions 2–3 extra touches allowed defenders to recover before she could shoot or release.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Earlier decision-making in the final third. Continue playing striker — she is most dangerous in advanced positions.
soccerCoach_teamNotes.Ruby:
SEASON NOTES – 2025

G5 vs Toowong White (W 3–1): First time playing wing/left-sided attacker. Handled the transition well — natural instinct still to drop back into defensive positions, which is understandable. Defensively protected the ball strongly and showed composure. Development area: distribution after winning space.

G6 vs Mitchelton Glitter (W 1–0): Started strongly and showed she can hold her own further up the field. Positive spell on the left wing — has put her hand up for more attacking opportunities.

G9 vs Springfield (W 6–3): Outstanding contribution at both ends of the field. Defended strongly and was rewarded with a goal. Excellent determination and commitment. Players of the Match.

G10 vs Bardon Bandits (W 3–2): Terrific team-first performance. Volunteered to play goalkeeper in the first half — made several good saves and distributed quickly down the wings (exactly as coached). Returned to defence in second half, composed performance.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Continue developing comfort in wider attacking positions. Already strong in defence — keep pushing into attack when the opportunity arises.
soccerCoach_teamNotes.Mia:
SEASON NOTES – 2025

G6 vs Mitchelton Glitter (W 1–0): Spent time in several different positions throughout the match, adapted well to each role. Despite constant changes, delivered another dependable performance. Players of the Match.

G7 vs Sapphires (D 4–4): Outstanding throughout. Consistently won possession, carried ball forward and made ground when others struggled. Scored one goal and was heavily involved across the field. Coach's view: would have been Player of the Match based on overall impact.

G9 vs Springfield (W 6–3): Excellent work rate and team-first attitude. Adapted well to a role outside her preferred position. Players of the Match.

G10 vs Bardon Bandits (W 3–2): A quieter game than we've come to expect. Didn't have as much influence on proceedings today but continued to work hard for the team.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Versatility is a strength — she has shown she can play multiple positions. Continue developing goal threat from midfield.
soccerCoach_teamNotes.Charlotte:
SEASON NOTES – 2025

G5 vs Toowong White (W 3–1): Played in right defence but tended to roam across the field and drift to the left. Observation: she may currently be more naturally suited to midfield, where her movement and desire to get involved can be used more effectively.

G6 vs Mitchelton Glitter (W 1–0): Solid game, contributed well whenever called upon.

G7 vs Sapphires (D 4–4): Looked uncomfortable playing on the wing and frequently drifted back into midfield. Positional discipline is a development area.

G10 vs Bardon Bandits (W 3–2): One of the standout performers from our regular squad. Excellent work rate, constantly involved around the ball, kept making herself available. A really energetic and influential game. Players of the Match.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Best in midfield — use her energy and involvement there. Work on positional discipline in wide positions when deployed there.
soccerCoach_teamNotes.Naomi:
SEASON NOTES – 2025

G6 vs Mitchelton Glitter (W 1–0): Continued to provide reliable effort, recognised for another strong team performance. Players of the Match.

G7 vs Sapphires (D 4–4): Showed plenty of energy but struggled to maintain positional discipline — often roaming away from her assigned side of the field.

G10 vs Bardon Bandits (W 3–2): Handled second-half goalkeeper duties well. Produced one outstanding diving save showing real commitment. The two late goals knocked her confidence a little, but they came during a difficult team spell rather than being a reflection of her performance.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Positional discipline — stay on her assigned side of the field. Building confidence in goal — the diving save showed real ability.
soccerCoach_teamNotes.Simone:
SEASON NOTES – 2025

G5 vs Toowong White (W 3–1): Played in goal during the second half, worked hard.

G7 vs Sapphires (D 4–4): Difficult afternoon in defence, found it challenging to deal with the attacking pressure. Continued to work hard and never stopped trying. Was awarded Player of the Match based on team rotation (not necessarily match performance).

G9 vs Springfield (W 6–3): Played on the left wing despite it not being her preferred position. Worked tirelessly throughout — excellent work rate and team-first attitude. Her effort and willingness to play for the team had a significant impact on the result. Players of the Match.

G10 vs Bardon Bandits (W 3–2): A quieter game than we've come to expect. Less influence on proceedings today but continued to work hard for the team.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Continue developing comfort in varied positions. Work rate and attitude are already excellent — channel that into positional discipline.
soccerCoach_teamNotes.Kiera:
SEASON NOTES – 2025

G5 vs Toowong White (W 3–1): Outstanding defensive performance. Critical to team's success. Consistently tracked back well, positioned between attacker and goal, disrupted opposition attacks and repeatedly won the ball back. Made several crucial defensive clearances near the goal line — became last line of defence multiple times. Very composed, reliable and intelligent.

G6 vs Mitchelton Glitter (W 1–0): Rock solid at the back. Consistently tracked back to protect the goal, supported the goalkeeper and provided important defensive cover whenever needed.

G7 vs Sapphires (D 4–4): Solid as always, providing reliability and composure at the back. Strong defensive game.

G9 vs Springfield (W 6–3): One of her strongest defensive performances of the season. Read the game well, tracked runners consistently and made several important defensive interventions. Particularly effective recovering to cover Springfield's pace out wide. Players of the Match.

G10 vs Bardon Bandits (W 3–2): Outstanding in defence again. Positioning and ability to get between attacker and goal continues to be exceptional. The timing of the opposition's two late goals (shortly after she rotated off) highlighted how important she is to our defensive organisation.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Continue as the anchor of our defensive unit. Kiera is already one of our most consistent performers — look for opportunities to bring her forward into attack.
soccerCoach_teamNotes.Nixie:
SEASON NOTES – 2025

G5 vs Toowong White (W 3–1): Very strong defensive game. Aggressive in getting to loose balls first — excellent job stepping forward to meet the ball early and clearing it out of our half. Her willingness to attack the ball defensively helped relieve pressure and prevented Toowong from building sustained attacks.

G7 vs Sapphires (D 4–4): Strong defensive game, worked hard throughout. Awarded Player of the Match based on team rotation.

G10 vs Bardon Bandits (W 3–2): An off day by her usual high standards. Looked a little less engaged than normal and wasn't able to influence the game as she typically does. Every player has those days — she'll bounce back.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Continue the aggressive, ball-winning defensive approach that has been a consistent strength. When having a difficult day, focus on the basics: get tight, attack the ball.
soccerCoach_teamNotes.Sienna:
SEASON NOTES – 2025 (Goalkeeper)

G5 vs Toowong White (W 3–1): Particularly impressive in goal. Showed real commitment and bravery. Willingness to dive for the ball and fully commit made a big impact and helped settle the team defensively.

G6 vs Mitchelton Glitter (W 1–0): Great composure in goal. After an attacker closed her down quickly, produced an excellent save to deny what looked like a certain goal. Also adapted well to team's goalkeeper expectations and continued to improve distribution and decision-making.

G7 vs Sapphires (D 4–4): Performed very well in goal, making several important saves and giving confidence to the team defensively. Learning opportunity: one opposition goal came from a corner that rolled through without being cleared — work on defending set pieces and reacting quickly inside the penalty area.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Distribution and quick decision-making when coming off the line. Defending corners and set pieces — organising defenders and reacting to loose balls in the box. Already showing excellent bravery and commitment.
soccerCoach_teamNotes.Poppy:
SEASON NOTES – 2025

G5 vs Toowong White (W 3–1): Strong defensively with some important clearances and good commitment. Supported the defensive line well and helped move the ball away from danger areas effectively.

G6 vs Mitchelton Glitter (W 1–0): Outstanding match in defence. Playing on the left side, was aggressive in winning the ball, stepped into contests confidently and repeatedly cleared danger — helped turn defence into attack. One of her strongest games of the season. Players of the Match.

G7 vs Sapphires (D 4–4): Strong defensive game, worked hard throughout.

DEVELOPMENT FOCUS: Continue the aggressive, attack-the-ball approach in defence — it has been her standout quality. Look for opportunities to get forward when the team has possession.
